summary: Implement M5 two-group split editing, orientation switching, cross-group tab movement, independent editor sessions, and focused-group render targeting.

decisions.decision: Keep group membership, tab order, orientation, focus, and per-group CodeMirror sessions in bounded UI state while retaining the existing document workspace as the single buffer/dirty-history authority.
decisions.rationale: FR-15.7 is a presentation and focus capability; duplicating document content or command history into a second application store would create conflicting save and undo ownership.

decisions.decision: Every group focus or tab activation dispatches the ordinary activate-document command. Render, export, diagnostics, history, and external navigation continue to consume the one active document, while group reconciliation updates synchronously from that runtime state.
decisions.rationale: This makes the render target follow focus without a parallel engine path and prevents a stale editor from receiving commands during diagnostic/history activations.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-15.7 requires two editor groups, side-by-side or stacked, with independent tabs and cross-group drag.
testEvidence.test: Exercise pure group split/orientation/move/reconcile/merge state and the rendered two-group tab UI with a synthetic cross-group drag.

red.outcome: RED: the editor-group state module did not exist.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/layout/editor-groups.test.ts tests/ui/editor/EditorGroupsPane.test.tsx tests/ui/editor/DocumentTabBar.test.tsx tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx
green.outcome: GREEN: 4 files and 33 tests passed, including the legacy tab/session/diagnostic behaviors.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-15.7 requires the render target to follow the focused group.
testEvidence.test: Disable auto-render, focus each group in turn, invoke preview, and inspect the actual EngineService request entry file.

red.outcome: RED was established by the pre-feature Workbench, which exposed only one editor panel and had no Split editor control.

green.outcome: GREEN: the focused primary request used parts/wheel.scad and the focused secondary request used main.scad.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
